generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryCraneController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "A PLC or embedded system interfacing with gantry crane actuators and sensors via industrial protocols (CAN bus, Modbus) for real-time operation"
]

instance VentilationActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Electronic actuators for greenhouse ventilation systems controlled via ActiveMQ for real-time adjustments"
]

instance ShadingActuator : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Motorized shading systems for greenhouse environmental control, managed through adaptive middleware"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position sensors (encoders) for real-time tracking of gantry crane crossbeam and hoist mechanisms"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionSensorData
]

instance VelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Velocity sensors measuring gantry crane's motion dynamics (forward/inverse kinematics) at high frequency"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VelocitySensorData
]

instance TorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensors monitoring actuator loads and motor wear for fault prediction"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueSensorData
]

instance LoadCell : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Load cells measuring payload distribution and weight capacity compliance"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LoadCellData
]

instance KinematicController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded controller interfacing with PLC for real-time kinematic calculations and actuator coordination"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ControllerStatusData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance PositionSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoder
]

instance VelocitySensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VelocitySensor
]

instance TorqueSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensor
]

instance LoadCellData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LoadCell
]

instance ControllerStatusData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom KinematicController
]

instance RealTimeTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoder, VelocitySensor, TorqueSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Mathematical model describing gantry crane's motion dynamics (forward/inverse kinematics) implemented in MATLAB/Simulink or Python (NumPy/SciPy)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBDatabase
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model predictive control framework for optimizing gantry crane trajectories considering kinematic limits and operational objectives"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ModelPredictiveControlEngine
]

instance FaultPredictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model using historical data to forecast potential failures (motor wear, bearing degradation) before they occur"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionService
]

instance PerformancePredictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Statistical model forecasting system performance metrics (cycle time, energy consumption) under varying operational conditions"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerformanceAnalyticsService
]

instance HistoricalDataArchive : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing high-resolution (100Hz+) historical data logs of physical system parameters (position, velocity, torque, energy consumption)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionSensorData, VelocitySensorData, TorqueSensorData, LoadCellData, ControllerStatusData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InfluxDBDatabase
]

instance RealTimeDataStream : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Continuous real-time data stream of physical system parameters for immediate validation and control purposes"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RealTimeTrajectoryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTBroker
]

// C11: Enablers
instance InfluxDBDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database for storing historical data logs at high resolution (100Hz+) for continuous validation requirements"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataArchive, ModelPredictiveControlEngine
]

instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message broker (MQTT) for real-time data exchange between physical sensors and digital twin services with low-latency requirements"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataStream
]

instance ModelPredictiveControlEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software framework implementing model predictive control algorithms for trajectory optimization and adaptive control"
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryOptimizationService
]

instance FaultDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning-based service for real-time fault detection and prediction using statistical process control methods"
    DTDFVocab:enables FaultPredictionService
]

instance PerformanceAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Data processing framework analyzing performance metrics for predictive maintenance and optimization recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformancePredictionService
]

instance ContainerizedDeployment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Containerization platform (Docker/Kubernetes) enabling modular deployment of digital twin components with version control flexibility"
]

instance AutomationMLStandard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Industrial interoperability standard ensuring standardized representation of digital twin components for cross-system integration"
]

instance DarTwinNotation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital Twin notation system for tracking system evolution and architectural changes over time"
]

// C6: Services
instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous recording of physical system parameters (position, velocity, torque, energy consumption) at real-time or high-frequency intervals"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataArchive
]

instance RealTimeStateVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D/2D visualization of gantry crane's kinematic state using CAD-based or kinematic model overlays"
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integration of statistical process control or machine learning-based approaches to identify deviations from expected behavior"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultAlerts
]

instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computational algorithms optimizing crane trajectories, payload distribution, or energy efficiency based on real-time constraints"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimization
]

instance TrajectoryPlanningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic generation of safe and efficient paths for gantry's movable components considering kinematic limits and operational objectives"
    DTDFVocab:provides PathPlanningService
]

instance FaultPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Use of historical data and machine learning models to forecast potential failures before they occur"
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveMaintenanceAlerts
]

instance PerformancePredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Forecasting system performance metrics (cycle time, energy consumption) under varying operational conditions"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceMetricsAnalysis
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Bidirectional feedback loop between digital twin's control algorithms and physical crane's controller for real-time synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustments
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison between physical crane's actual behavior and its digital twin model using kinematic discrepancies"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationMetrics
]

instance TwinVersionManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ad-hoc version control and containerization management for modular deployment of digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:provides DeploymentManagement
]

instance InteroperabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Ensures standardized communication between digital twin components and external systems via AutomationML"
    DTDFVocab:provides CrossSystemIntegration
]

instance EvolutionTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Tracks changes in physical/digital twin architecture using DarTwin notation for system evolution management"
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureChangeTracking
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance KinematicAlignmentInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time comparison of physical and digital twin kinematic states showing position/velocity/torque discrepancies"
]

instance TrajectoryOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimal path recommendations for gantry crane operations considering kinematic constraints and payload requirements"
]

instance FaultPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted failure probabilities for critical components (motors, bearings) based on historical data patterns"
]

instance PerformanceTrendInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Forecasted performance metrics (cycle time, energy consumption) under current operational conditions"
]

instance ControlParameterAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of control parameters based on real-time sensor feedback and digital twin simulations"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance TrajectoryReoptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic recalibration of crane trajectories when new operational constraints are detected"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FaultAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggered when anomaly detection exceeds predefined thresholds, sending alerts for maintenance intervention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ValidationThresholdAlert : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alert system when digital twin validation metrics exceed acceptable error thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationRecommendation : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Provides actionable recommendations for improving system performance based on predictive analytics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DeploymentUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Containerized deployment updates to incorporate new digital twin components or version improvements"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C17: Environment
instance LabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryCraneController, PositionEncoder, VelocitySensor, TorqueSensor, LoadCell
]

instance ResearchLab : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains KinematicModel, TrajectoryOptimizationModel, FaultPredictionModel, PerformancePredictionModel, HistoricalDataArchive
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains RealTimeDataStream, InfluxDBDatabase, ModelPredictiveControlEngine, FaultDetectionService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the digital twin involves **command flows** from the digital components to the **Physical Crane** via the **Control Application** and **Physical Crane Interface**. Key aspects include:

1. **Command Types**: The system generates **trajectory commands** (e.g., waypoints, velocity profiles) via the **Trajectory Generator Service**, which ensures **optimal paths** with minimal swinging motion. These commands are validated by the **Simulation Service** before execution.

2. **Control Message Formats**: Commands are likely formatted as **structured payloads** (e.g., JSON or binary) over MQTT, with **command IDs** and **timestamp stamps** for sequencing. The **Control Application** translates these into low-level control signals for the crane’s controllers.

3. **Validation and Authentication**:
   - The **Continuous Validation Service** preemptively checks if commands align with simulated trajectories, rejecting invalid or unsafe commands (e.g., those exceeding physical limits).
   - **Conflict Resolution**: If multiple services (e.g., operator overrides, safety protocols) trigger conflicting commands, the **Control Application** may implement **priority-based resolution** (e.g., operator commands take precedence over automated trajectories).

4. **Feedback Systems**: The **Physical Crane Interface** provides **acknowledgment signals** (e.g., success/failure flags) to the **Control Application** upon command execution, ensuring the digital twin is updated with real-time status (e.g., 'command executed' vs. 'timeout').

5. **Command Execution Confirmation**: The **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** displays **execution status** (e.g., 'Moving to Waypoint X') and **feedback metrics** (e.g., actual vs. planned trajectory deviations) to the operator, enabling closed-loop control.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
  The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar demonstrates **real-time behavioral adaptation** with **synchronization between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin**, leveraging **InfluxDB for time-series data storage** and **ActiveMQ for message broker communication**. While exact synchronization frequencies are not specified, the system implies **low-latency (<100ms) updates** due to its self-adaptive nature, where both physical and digital components dynamically adjust based on sensor inputs and pre-recorded data. The **time scale for services** appears to be **real-time to near-real-time**, with emphasis on **adaptive response cycles** (e.g., sensor-triggered adjustments).

  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar focuses on **runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction** with **Microsoft Azure tools**, suggesting **sub-second latency** for critical air quality metrics (e.g., CO₂, PM2.5) via **Raspberry-Pi sensor integration**. The **temporal granularity** is likely **high-frequency (e.g., 1–10 Hz)** for real-time monitoring, with **prediction models** operating on **hourly/real-time aggregated data** for virus spread prevention applications. AutomationML-based modeling enables **model-driven synchronization**, but specific time scales remain unspecified.

  **Not in Document** for the lab-scale gantry crane system regarding temporal aspects (e.g., synchronization frequencies, latency requirements).
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
  The **GreenhouseDT** exemplifies a **self-adaptive constellation** where the digital twin **behaviorally mirrors the physical greenhouse** via **InfluxDB/ActiveMQ-driven synchronization**. The **orchestration pattern** is **ad-hoc**, relying on **containerized VMs** with pre-recorded data for baseline adaptation. **Resource management** is implicit but likely optimized via **virtual machine isolation** and **InfluxDB time-series scaling**.

  The **Air Quality Management** system employs a **Model-Driven Digital Twin (MDDT) constellation**, integrating **AutomationML** for standardized modeling and **Microsoft Azure** for cloud orchestration. **Component integration** includes **Raspberry-Pi sensor fusion** with **Azure IoT Edge** for edge processing. **System-wide coordination** occurs via **Azure’s service mesh** (e.g., for runtime visualization/prediction), with **no explicit orchestration framework** beyond MDDT principles.

  The **lab-scale gantry crane** system uses **containerization/virtualization** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) for modular twin components, visualized via **C4 Model diagrams**. **Architecture patterns** are **ad-hoc**, with **resource management** likely via **container orchestration tools** (e.g., Kubernetes for scalability). **Coordination** is implied through **C4-compliant microservices**, but no specialized twin constellation framework is mentioned.

  **Not in Document** for any commercial digital twin framework (e.g., Siemens MindSphere, PTC ThingWorx) usage, reinforcing an **ad-hoc constellation** approach.
  "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar outlines a **self-adaptive digital twin lifecycle** focused on **behavioral adaptation** of both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. While the document does not explicitly detail formal lifecycle stages, the system leverages **pre-recorded virtual machine (VM) images** with embedded data for deployment, suggesting a **pre-configured, pre-deployment phase** where infrastructure is set up with standardized InfluxDB databases and ActiveMQ message brokers. The lifecycle governance appears **ad-hoc**, relying on containerized virtual machines for modular deployment and easy scalability.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar, while not explicitly detailing lifecycle phases, implies a **model-driven lifecycle** where the digital twin is built using **AutomationML** for structured representation. The system integrates with **Microsoft Azure tools**, suggesting a **cloud-based lifecycle** with runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction phases. The lifecycle governance is implied to be **model-driven engineering (MDE)-aligned**, with a focus on **interoperability via AutomationML standards** and **real-time sensor data processing** (Raspberry-Pi + sensors) for adaptive management.

No explicit lifecycle transitions or governance frameworks (e.g., CMMI, Agile, DevOps) are mentioned, but the **ad-hoc, domain-specific approaches** (e.g., containerization, virtualization) imply iterative development cycles with **sensor-driven validation** at runtime.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar addresses fidelity and validity through:
- **Model fidelity requirements**:
  - **Behavioral adaptation** ensures digital twin mirrors physical greenhouse dynamics (e.g., thermal inertia, sensor noise).
  - **InfluxDB** enables high-fidelity time-series analysis for model validation.
- **Validation/verification methods**:
  - **Self-adaptive loops**: Digital twin continuously validates against physical system behavior (e.g., temperature deviations trigger recalibration).
  - **Pre-recorded data**: VM images serve as ground truth for offline validation.
- **Uncertainty quantification**:
  - **ActiveMQ** ensures low-latency data integrity; **InfluxDB** supports statistical analysis of sensor noise.
- **Error detection/correction**:
  - **Adaptive middleware** detects discrepancies (e.g., sensor drift) and triggers corrections (e.g., actuator adjustments).
- **Quality assurance**:
  - **Containerized deployment** ensures reproducibility; **VM images** guarantee consistent environments.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar includes:
- **Model fidelity requirements**:
  - **AutomationML** ensures compliance with industrial standards (e.g., ISO 15118 for IoT interoperability).
  - **Azure IoT Edge** processes data with **edge computing** to reduce latency and preserve fidelity.
- **Validation/verification methods**:
  - **Runtime visualization** (Power BI) cross-checks against physical sensor data.
  - **Simulation/prediction models** are validated via **Azure Machine Learning** pipelines.
- **Uncertainty quantification**:
  - **Azure IoT Hub** logs sensor telemetry for anomaly detection (e.g., outliers flagged as potential errors).
- **Error detection/correction**:
  - **AutomationML safety constraints** enforce fail-safes (e.g., emergency stop if air quality exceeds thresholds).
- **Quality assurance**:
  - **Model-driven engineering** ensures consistency across runtime, simulation, and prediction models.

**Not in Document** for the lab-scale gantry crane exemplar regarding fidelity/validity considerations (only architecture context is provided).
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s technical network infrastructure** leverages **MQTT** as the primary communication protocol for inter-service messaging, enabling low-latency, event-driven interactions. Key technical details include:

1. **Communication Protocols**: MQTT (version unspecified but likely v3.1.1 or v5.0) is used for:
   - **Publish-Subscribe Model**: Services (e.g., Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service) publish data topics (e.g., `crane/trajectory/plan`, `crane/measurement/real-time`) to the **MQTT Broker**.
   - **QoS Levels**: Likely **QoS 1 (at least once delivery)** for critical commands (e.g., trajectory updates) to ensure reliability.
   - **Security**: Not explicitly mentioned, but in practice, MQTT could use **TLS/SSL** for encrypted communication (implied by real-world adoption).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**: The system operates in a **centralized, star-topology** where the **MQTT Broker** acts as the central hub, connecting all services (e.g., Control Application, Logging Application, Dashboard) to the **Physical Crane Interface**. The **Physical Crane** itself is assumed to be directly interfaced via a **local network** (e.g., Ethernet or Wi-Fi).

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Trajectory Generation/Validation**: Requires **low-latency (<100 ms)** for real-time adjustments, with bandwidth sufficient for **100 Hz+ data streams** (e.g., 10–20 KB/s for sensor data).
   - **Dashboard Visualization**: The **Grafana Dashboard** likely consumes **real-time streaming data** with minimal latency (<50 ms) for operator monitoring.

4. **Reliability and Redundancy**:
   - **MQTT Broker**: Implicit redundancy may exist if the broker is deployed on a **high-availability cluster** (e.g., Mosquitto with persistent storage).
   - **Physical Crane Interface**: Designed to operate **standalone** (without digital twin), implying fault tolerance for hardware failures.

5. **Security Protocols**: Not explicitly detailed, but the system could employ:
   - **Authentication**: Service-to-service credentials (e.g., MQTT client IDs) or API keys.
   - **Authorization**: Role-based access control (e.g., operator vs. researcher) via the **HMI** or **Logging Application**.
   - **Data Integrity**: MQTT’s built-in acknowledgments and message IDs could prevent replay attacks.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s hosting and deployment infrastructure** is designed for **scalability, real-time performance, and edge-computing capabilities**, with the following specifications:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: The **Physical Crane Interface**, **Control Application**, and **Logging Application** are likely deployed on **edge devices** (e.g., Raspberry Pi, NVIDIA Jetson) to minimize latency for real-time control.
   - **Cloud/On-Premise**: The **Database (TimescaleDB)**, **Dashboard (Grafana)**, **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, and **Continuous Validation Service** are hosted on a **centralized server** (on-premise or cloud-based), with **TimescaleDB** optimized for time-series data.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **CPU/Memory**: The **Simulation Service** and **Trajectory Generator Service** require **multi-core CPUs** (e.g., 4+ cores) and **high RAM** (e.g., 16–32 GB) to handle real-time kinematics simulations and trajectory optimizations.
   - **Storage**: **TimescaleDB** stores **historical sensor data** (e.g., 100 Hz sampling for 1 hour = ~3.6 MB of raw data) with **compression** (e.g., TimescaleDB’s built-in compression) to reduce storage footprint.
   - **Bandwidth**: The **MQTT Broker** and **Logging Application** must support **high-throughput data ingestion** (e.g., 100+ messages/sec) with **low-latency persistence** to the database.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Horizontal Scaling**: Services like the **Dashboard** or **Continuous Validation Service** could be scaled via **containerization** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) to handle concurrent operator/researcher workloads.
   - **Database Scaling**: **TimescaleDB** supports **sharding** and **partitioning** to distribute load across multiple nodes for large-scale deployments.
   - **Load Balancing**: The **MQTT Broker** may use **load balancing** (e.g., HAProxy) to distribute traffic across multiple brokers.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Microservices Architecture**: The digital twin is structured as **modular services** (e.g., Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service) deployed independently, enabling **easy updates** and **feature isolation**.
   - **Containerization**: While not explicitly mentioned, the **C4 Model** suggests services could be containerized (e.g., Docker) for portability and orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes).

5. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
   - **High Availability**: The **Database** and **MQTT Broker** are designed for **redundancy** (e.g., failover clusters) to ensure uptime during hardware failures.
   - **Fault Tolerance**: The **Continuous Validation Service** includes **threshold-based alerts** to prevent cascading failures (e.g., if a sensor fails, the system notifies operators before critical errors occur).
   - **Disaster Recovery**: Not detailed, but the system could implement **backups** for the **TimescaleDB** database and **service rollback** procedures for critical failures.
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
  The **GreenhouseDT** system integrates **externally via InfluxDB/ActiveMQ** for data exchange with other IoT devices (e.g., sensors) but **no explicit interoperability standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) are detailed. **Dependencies** are limited to **pre-recorded data in VMs**, with **no mention of external enterprise systems**.

  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar integrates **externally via AutomationML** for interoperability with construction engineering tools and **Microsoft Azure** for cloud-based services. **Data exchange protocols** include **Azure IoT Edge** for edge-to-cloud sync, with **no explicit standards** beyond AutomationML. **Dependencies** extend to **Azure services** (e.g., visualization, prediction) but **no peer-to-peer twin integration** is described.

  The **lab-scale gantry crane** system’s **horizontal integration** is implied via **C4 Model diagrams**, suggesting **containerized microservices** for modularity but **no explicit external system dependencies**. **Interoperability** may rely on **standardized protocols** (e.g., OPC UA) if extended, though not documented. **Coordination with other Digital Twins** is **not addressed**, reinforcing an **ad-hoc, domain-specific** approach.

  **Not in Document** for any explicit mention of **standardized protocols** (e.g., MQTT, OPC UA) or **enterprise system integrations** (e.g., ERP, SCADA).
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar does **not** explicitly adopt industry standards. Its infrastructure relies on **common technologies** (InfluxDB, ActiveMQ, VMs) but lacks formal compliance frameworks. The **ad-hoc, low-cost design** prioritizes ease of deployment over standardization.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar **complies with AutomationML (AutomationML)** for digital twin representation, ensuring **interoperability** between sensors (Raspberry-Pi), the digital twin, and cloud platforms (Microsoft Azure). AutomationML’s **OPC UA foundation** enables seamless data exchange, while Azure’s **IoT Hub and Logic Apps** integrate with industry standards like **OPC UA, MQTT, and REST APIs**. No explicit certification requirements (e.g., ISO 27001, IEC 61508) are mentioned, but the **construction engineering context** suggests alignment with **building information modeling (BIM) standards** (e.g., IFC).

The **LAB SCALE GANTRY CRANE** (C4 Model context) does **not** mention standardization frameworks. Its twinning process appears **domain-specific**, relying on **C4 Model diagrams** for architecture visualization rather than formal standards. The **cyber-physical system** may implicitly follow **IEC 61499** (for process control) or **OPC UA** (for interoperability), but these are not explicitly referenced.

**Key standardization gaps**:
- **GreenhouseDT**: None (ad-hoc, no standards).
- **Air Quality Management**: **AutomationML (OPC UA foundation), Azure IoT standards (OPC UA, MQTT, REST)**.
- **LAB SCALE GANTRY CRANE**: **None (ad-hoc, C4 Model only)**.
"
]
